q: Which Roman emperor made Christianity legal?
exp: The Edict of Milan (313 AD) allowed Christian worship in the Empire.

q: In which year did Constantinople fall to the Ottomans?
exp: Its fall marked the end of the Byzantine Empire.

q: Who wrote the epic poem 'The Lusiads'?
exp: Camões published 'Os Lusíadas' in 1572, about the Age of Discovery.

q: Which pre-Columbian civilization had its capital at Tenochtitlan?
exp: Tenochtitlan stood where Mexico City is today.

q: Which revolution began in Russia in 1917?
exp: It led to the creation of the Soviet Union.

q: Which treaty ended World War I in 1919?
exp: Its harsh terms on Germany are often linked to the rise of WWII.

q: Who was the first man to walk on the Moon, in 1969?
exp: Armstrong stepped onto the Moon during the Apollo 11 mission.

q: Which empire was led by Genghis Khan?
exp: It became the largest contiguous land empire in history.

q: Which document declared the independence of the USA in 1776?
exp: It was largely drafted by Thomas Jefferson.

q: Which plague devastated Europe in the 14th century?
exp: The Black Death killed up to a third of Europe's population.

q: Who was the first Roman emperor?
exp: Augustus became emperor in 27 BC after Julius Caesar's death.

q: Which 1648 peace ended the Thirty Years' War?
exp: It shaped the modern idea of sovereign nation-states.
